programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: In the early 1980s the next battlefield in the struggle between communism and democracy, emerges in the United States own backyard. The Cold War is an arms race, a propaganda battle and at times a deadly game.

programme.title: M.A.D. World
programme.desc: In 1989, the USSR pulls out of Afghanistan and the last remaining proxy conflict of the Cold War ends. A new era emerges as brutal barriers are broken down. A peaceful transition is what the watching world prays for, but is by no means a sure thing.

programme.title: Oppenheimer
programme.desc: Robert Oppenheimer leads the effort to develop the atom bomb during the Second World War. When revealed, his work initially makes him a hero in the United States, but a changing world soon sees him recast as a security risk.

programme.title: The Blitz on The North
programme.desc: Documentary focusing on how the German Luftwaffe and flying bombs caused devastation and chaos across the north of England and parts of Scotland.

programme.title: The Blitz On The Midlands
programme.desc: A documentary assessing the effect of the concerted German bombing of cities and towns in the midlands during World War Two.

programme.title: Hitler vs Stalin
programme.desc: Historians analyse the Battle for Stalingrad, focusing on Hitler, Stalin, and Zhukovs campaigns.

programme.title: Hitler vs Stalin
programme.desc: Historians analyse the Battle for Stalingrad, focusing on Hitler, Stalin, and Zhukovs campaigns.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: In the run-up to September 1939, political and economic chaos in the aftermath of World War I allowed Hitler in Germany, Mussolini in Italy and ultra-nationalists in imperial Japan to seize absolute power.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Germany took just four weeks to conquer Poland, using a new form of warfare - Blitzkrieg. Six armoured tank units known as Panzer divisions, supported by the Luftwaffe, quickly broke through Polish defences.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: After the retreat from Dunkirk, Churchill rallied Britain in its darkest hour. Hitler was only just across the channel, but he reckoned without our supreme strategist, the might of the navy and the manoeuvrability of the air force.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Explores Hitlers assault on the Soviet Union in the summer of 1941, Operation Barbarossa and his attack on the Caucasus in 1942.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The attack on Pearl Harbour brought the sleeping giant, America, into the war, and saw the emergence of the aircraft carrier as the pre-eminent weapon of the seas.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Mussolini wanted to recreate the Roman Empire, but proved to be a poor military strategist and his incompetence contributed to Hitlers eventual defeat.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: With their armies fully engaged on the battlegrounds, the Allied and Axis powers focused their efforts to develop new weapons and new strategies to deliver a knockout blow to the enemy and bring about a quick end to the conflict.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: War on the two fronts caught up with Hitler leaving him on the run in mid 1943.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: Barbarossa: The Lost Diaries
programme.desc: Lieutenant Sander undertakes an arduous journey to Leningrad, revealing the harsh German east campaign of Operation Barbarossa. He notes down his experiences in a diary that provides us a look at the attack on the Soviet Union.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: By early 1944, Hitler had long been expecting an Allied attack on north-western France, but his problem was knowing when and where it would come. This episode takes a look at the Allied operations Bodyguard and Overload.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Concentrates on Hitlers attempts in the latter end of 1944 to shift the balance of power back in his favour.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: The war in the Pacific against Japan was a savage hand-to-hand, island-to-island struggle, fought by new specialist fighting units like the valiant Chindits.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Allied forces converge on Germany, uncovering the horrors of Belsen and Buchenwald, before pushing towards Berlin, where Hitler clings to power in his last desperate days.

programme.title: World War II in Colour
programme.desc: Explores Hitlers assault on the Soviet Union in the summer of 1941, Operation Barbarossa and his attack on the Caucasus in 1942.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: The team is in the north of Britain where they uncover evidence of the first Roman siege in Britain, Scottish man-made islands and a famous monastery.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The British Win Every War?
programme.desc: Its 865 AD and the Great Heathen Army, the Vikings, have invaded Britain. Al takes up his sword to discover the truth behind how the Vikings were so successful in Britain. With John Thomson, they explore the truth of what Vikings really looked like.

programme.title: Al Murray: Why Do The Brits Win Every War?
programme.desc: Al meets the Auld Enemy, where bitter sibling rivalry is as strong today as its ever been. Joined by Sanjeev Kohli, Al visits Craufurdland Castle to explore the truth behind famous battles from the Wars of Scottish Independence.

programme.title: Two Sides Of History
programme.sub-title: They Called Me A Granny Killer
programme.desc: One says lockdowns destroyed lives. The other says they saved them. A doctor and a critic debate: did Britain go too far-or not far enough? As the dust settles, they face the big q.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The River Hunters head to the River Lochy in search for evidence of the clashing Scottish clans embroiled in the 17th century civil war.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits Lancaster Castle to explore its grisly history of crime and punishment, including the infamous Lancashire Witches trials and the martyrdom of the Jesuit priest Saint Edmund Arrowsmith.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: Dan Jones visits the heart of the English countryside to explore Arundel Castle and the dramatic history of the Earls of Arundel.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: From an Anglo-Saxon Burial ground to the caves of the Judean Desert and Siberia in the Ice Age, uncover the origins of the curses surrounding Sutton Hoo, Lyuba and more.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: From the time of the Knights Templar to pre-historic Spain, uncover the origins of the curses surrounding the Turin Shroud, the Cave of Altamira and more.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Finds include: revelations from `Britains Pompeii, the 3,000-year-old perfectly preserved village in Cambridgeshire, including how Bronze Age ancestors designed their homes, and their kitchens packed with food and equipment, and the theatre where Shakespeare premiered Romeo and Juliet and Henry V.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.sub-title: Viking Camp And Iron Age Gold
programme.desc: A look at the camp from which Vikings invaded Britain, and groundbreaking evidence that the world-famous Avebury stone circle isnt just a sacred site but a place where people lived and worked. In Staffordshire, the oldest Iron Age gold in Britain is unearthed.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The River Hunters head to the River Lochy in search for evidence of the clashing Scottish clans embroiled in the 17th century civil war.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The River Hunters head to the cathedral city of Canterbury in Kent, where they are granted special permission to undertake the first official search of this section of the River Stour.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: In medieval England, the North was ruled from York. Dan Jones explores the history of its castle, from the Viking Jorvik and the Normans, through the dark days of the anti-Jewish pogrom and the imprisonment of the Knights Templar, to one of its most colourful inmates - the legendary highwayman Dick Turpin.

programme.title: Secrets of Great British Castles
programme.desc: With the aid of dramatic reconstruction, Dan Jones explores the 900-year history of the beautiful Leeds Castle in Kent, from its origins as a royal Saxon familys manor and Norman stronghold to the private property of six medieval queens, a Tudor palace, a Jacobean country house, a Georgian mansion and an elegant 20th-century retreat for the rich and famous.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads off for a 45-mile walk across Wiltshire to tell the story of life and death in the last centuries of the Stone Age. His route takes him through the greatest concentration of prehistoric sites in Europe.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: It was 30 years after the Romans invaded Britain that they were ready to take on the challenge of conquering the Lake District. Tony Robinson tracks their 50-mile walk from Penrith past Ullswater to Ambleside and on to the Irish Sea at Ravenglass.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: In the North, the remains of a Tudor house in Leicestershire were thought to be the childhood home of Englands forgotten Queen, Lady Jane Grey. But when archaeologists excavate, they find more than they bargained for.

programme.title: Digging for Britain
programme.desc: Find out how a lobster led archaeologists to the discovery of an 8,000-year-old Neolithic underwater settlement. Join a team at a secret location in Dorset as they excavate an Iron Age crouched burial and in Londons East End,location reporter Naoise Mac Sweeney visits a modern construction site as it gives up the secrets of an Elizabethan playhouse.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: From rural Spain in the 1800s to Russia during World War II, uncover the origins of the curses surrounding Goyas Black Paintings, the Amber Room and more.

programme.title: Cursed Treasures
programme.desc: Archaeologists and scientists help to uncover the truth behind the deadly curses bestowed on the finders of some of the worlds most fascinating treasures.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: The team find evidence from one of Scotlands bloodiest eras as they wade through the waters of the River Garry in the Scottish Highlands, the site of the first major clash between Highland Jacobites and the government army.

programme.title: River Hunters
programme.desc: Rick Edwards and Beau Ouimette embark upon an epic search through the River Ouse, which winds its way through North Yorkshire, in a bid to find evidence that it was once home to Viking life.

programme.title: Walking Through History
programme.desc: Tony Robinson sets off on his most curious walk yet, across the ancient and turbulent kingdom of Northumbria. In the seventh century, at the height of the Dark Ages, the rule of Northumbria stretched from Edinburgh to York.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin visit a one-bedroom flat in Catford, London, a large house near the River Mersey and a semi-detached property in Alvaston in Derby.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: An end-terrace house in Alfreton in Derby bought by two brothers, a mid-terrace on the coast at Seaham in County Durham and a large three-storey property with a flat and shop in Taylorstown in the Rhondda Valley are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin talk to the new owners to find out what they have planned for their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts falls in love with a quirky cottage in Malvern bought by a builder and his wife, Dion Dublin talks to a first-time property developer who sees her end-terrace house in Chell in Stoke-on-Trent as something for her pension pot, and Lucy Alexander meets a divorced couple who have decided to form a new union as property developers doing up a shop and flat in Dover, Kent.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A large piece of land in Ratby in Leicestershire, a terrace in Chesterton in Staffordshire and a 1930s semi with a huge garden and huge potential are all sold at auction. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the developers hoping to breathe new life into their auction buys.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A two-bedroom detached house in Basford in Nottingham, a top floor flat in Portsmouth and a large Edwardian terrace in Crewe are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion meet the new owners and hear about their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A multimillion-pound development in Farningham in Kent, an ambitious, total renovation project in Aspatria in Cumbria and a very small studio flat in Wallington in Surrey are all sold under the hammer. Martin and Lucy meet the new owners to discuss their plans.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A Victorian terrace in Londons Greenwich, two flats in Chatham in Kent and a riverside inn near Great Yarmouth in Norfolk are all sold under the hammer. Martin Roberts, Lucy Alexander and Dion Dublin meet the new owners and hear plans for their renovation projects.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A three-bedroom house in Borrowash in Derbyshire, another three-bedder in West Bromwich in the Midlands and a quirky four-bedroom house on the Isle of Sheppey are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A flat in Pegswood in Northumberland, a terrace in Swadlincote in Derbyshire and a former shop in Chatham in Kent are given makeovers.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A mid-terrace house in Wales, a basement office in central London with a huge price tag and two flats in Bootle near Liverpool are all sold under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Lucy meets a young couple expecting a baby who have to weigh up whether to live in their auction buy in Sydenham in London while Martin talks to a man and his father in law who have bought their first property together in Trentham near Stoke and Dion meets a man who only went into property development after he retired. His latest buy is a three-bed semi in West Bromwich.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Terraced houses in Tunstall in Shropshire and Prescott in Liverpool and a large flat in Dagenham are all sold under the hammer. Martin, Lucy and Dion have a look round before hearing what the new owners have planned.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A chiropodist, two part-time gardeners and a woman who works on projects in between the school run are todays investors. Theyve bought property in Bullbridge in Derbyshire, Newcastle under Lyme in Staffordshire and Wolverhampton in the West Midlands under the hammer.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts follows the progress of a couple developing a chalet bungalow in Drakelowe in Staffordshire for their family, while Lucy Alexander talks to a young entrepreneur with grand plans for a three-bed semi in Croydon in London. Dion Dublin looks round a piece of land which runs alongside a tourist steam train track in Coltishall in Norfolk.

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: The story of everyday life in a small and tightly-knit community - this is the core of the worlds longest running primetime drama serial and the highest-rating programme in the UK. Coronation Street has for over 50 years uniquely combined cult status with popularity and durability - the quality of writing is second to none, portraying believable characters with a large helping of humour. Its themes - love, passion, deception, greed and intrigue - are ones which everyone can relate to.

programme.title: Cowboy
programme.desc: A Chicago hotel clerk is dramatically transformed from tenderfoot to hardened trail hand when he goes on a cattle drive with a range boss he befriends. When the clerk loans the cowboy some money for a poker game, he is repaid with an invitation to join in a roundup. In the course of the eventful adventure, the clerks romantic notions of cowboy life in the Wild West are permanently dispelled.

programme.title: Donovans Reef
programme.desc: Michael Donovan, Thomas Gilhooley and William Dedham are all ex-Navy pals and have ended up on a South Pacific island after the war. Donovan runs the bar, Gilhooley hangs out there and Dedham is now the island doctor, married to one of the locals. Trouble threatens their paradise when Dedhams estranged daughter Amelia arrives with news that he is heir to the familys shipping company.

programme.title: Star Trek
programme.desc: A dark sense of foreboding falls over the crew of the USS Enterprise when the deserted hulk of their sister ship is found orbiting Omega IV. On board, Kirk discovers the crews uniforms covered with a strange white powder, and tracks down a starship captain on the planet surface.

programme.title: Vertical Limit
programme.desc: A photographer, who witnessed his famous mountaineer fathers death as a child, must face his fears and rescue his sister who is trapped on K2 at 26,000 feet. Feeling responsible for his fathers fatal accident, the young man had given up climbing, but when his sister and her team become trapped after an avalanche, he must enlist the help of experts in order to save the group.

programme.title: The Enforcer

programme.desc: Jake Donaldson is a male comedian who just wants to be a good feminist, but he grew up in the 90s and has a `proper man for a dad, so now he feels bad about not being able to put up a shelf.

programme.title: Njambi McGrath: Breaking Black
programme.desc: Njambi McGrath offers acerbic anecdotes about life in Kenya, living in a white neighbourhood, why immigrants are attracted to the UK, and how they thrive amongst certain hostilities.

programme.title: Becky Brunning: Bloom
programme.desc: Bright, breezy and a breath of fresh air, Beckys feel good story-telling on love, sexuality, feminism, and embarrassing dads is all charm.

programme.title: Eleanor Morton: Great Title, Glamorous Photo
programme.desc: Taking on the misogyny and double standards of the comedy industry, Eleanor Morton tries to reinvent herself and follow in her fathers footsteps as a glamorous American comedienne.

programme.title: Holly Burn: The Best of The Burn
programme.desc: Daredevil absurdist Holly Burn creates a whole host of obscure characters and semi-reckless stunts.

programme.title: Shayne Smith: Prison for Wizards
programme.desc: Comedian and former gang member, Shayne Smith performs in a stand-up special.

programme.title: Bob Smiley: Youre Just Hungry
programme.desc: Comedian Bob Smiley performs a standup set.

programme.title: Leanne Morgan: So Yummy
programme.desc: Leanne Morgan gives an honest look at suburban life and the challenges of keeping her husband happy while juggling kids, dogs, and cheerleading camp.

programme.title: BT: My Life as I Know It Now!
programme.desc: BTs physical performance style and charm landed him two performances.

programme.title: Andy Woodhull: Worlds Greatest Stepdad
programme.desc: This special premiered on Comedy Central after an appearance on Conan.

programme.title: Bone Hampton: It Is What It Is
programme.desc: As down home as collards and cornbread, as sharp as a pickaxe, and as funny as a possum in a petticoat, Comedian Bone Hampton is a master at making audiences feel like family.

programme.title: Spencer James: Bullies Are Necessary
programme.desc: Spencer has performed all over North America, he has won festivals, and he was recently featured on Foxs television show `Laughs, yet now, with the title of Las Vegas headliner, he brings all of his talents to the stage for a once-in-a-generation experience.

programme.title: Cleto Rodriguez: Cleto Is My Real Name
programme.desc: Rodriguez connects with audiences by discussing relatable topics, from marriage issues to the challenges of dieting.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Jeff Jena: Old Dog, New Jokes
programme.desc: Jeffrey Jena, one of the top corporate and cruise comics working today, performs his comedy.

programme.title: Jose Sarduy: Cuban Pilot
programme.desc: Joses wealth of life experiences lead to a roller coaster ride thats been his life.

programme.title: Andy Woodhull: Worlds Greatest Stepdad
programme.desc: This special premiered on Comedy Central after an appearance on Conan.

programme.title: Jeremy Nunes: Neighborhood Sasq-Watch
programme.desc: A perofrmance from Jeremy Nunes who draws rave reviews from critics and fans alike for his clever observations, unique storytelling style, and ability to improvise with the audience.

programme.title: Jeff Shaw: Manly Girly Man
programme.desc: Jeff Shaws writing is marked both by idealism and humanity. His stimulating satire is often infused with poetic beauty.

programme.title: Ty Barnett: Yep...
programme.desc: Comedian Ty Barnett performs.

programme.title: Jeff Capri: Capricious
programme.desc: The energetically laid back comic performs.

programme.title: Don Barnhart: The Spinal Disintergration of Man
programme.desc: Barnhart explores modern day society in a way that is hysterical and thought-provoking.

programme.title: David Dyer: Further Along Than Anyone Expected
programme.desc: Comedian David Dyer performs a standup set.

programme.title: Brian Hicks: Weird Mouth Things
programme.desc: The only thing Brian Hicks believes in any more is shortening the lines at Kohls; apart from that, Valentines Day cards can remain unread, dentists can remain mediocre, and the meaning of subdivision names can remain a mystery.

programme.title: Dennis Regan: Dennisland
programme.desc: Dennis Regan performs his signature brand of observational comedy.

programme.title: Drew Allen: Out of Ideas
programme.desc: Cursed with the ability to over-analyze even the most basic of human interactions, comedian Drew Allen has been delighting audiences with his distinct brand of anxious comedy since 2010.

programme.title: Ray Bradshaw: Deaf Comedy Fam
programme.desc: Ray Bradshaw tells a loveable tale of growing up with deaf parents, laced with pranks and playful, witty anecdotes of family life in his signature cheeky Glaswegian humour.

programme.title: Njambi McGrath: Breaking Black
programme.desc: Njambi McGrath offers acerbic anecdotes about life in Kenya, living in a white neighbourhood, why immigrants are attracted to the UK, and how they thrive amongst certain hostilities.

programme.title: Pete Heat: Blimey
programme.desc: Lanky urban wizard Pete Heat uses comedy and mind-blowing tricks to explore that most divisive of subjects: magic itself. Whether it is a visceral experience that loosens ones grasp on reality or a dated, cliche-riddled embarrassment, is TBD.

programme.title: Ali Brice: I tried to be funny but you werent looking
programme.desc: Ali Brice embraces his life after almost losing it. Three years on, hes admitted defeat and is back on his feet and trying again. Through therapy, friends and the magic of sausages.

programme.title: Sian Davies: About Time
programme.desc: Growing up to find ones place in the world: A metamorphic year of adventure, travel and trouble.

programme.title: Archie Maddocks: Matchstick
programme.desc: Cool, smart and humble to boot, Archie Maddocks presents a provocative, unflinching hour of life, love, loss and laughs with his iconic style of candid authenticity.

programme.title: Aaron Simmonds: Disabled Coconut
programme.desc: Wheelchair enthusiast Aaron Simmonds discusses being trolled online for not being disabled enough.

programme.title: Tarot: Cautionary Tales
programme.desc: Come learn life lessons from five people still doing sketch comedy in their 30s, by which they mean its a drain on their bank accounts and they dont talk to their parents about it.

programme.title: Gerry Kyei: NextUp Special
programme.desc: Anecdotal, observational and refreshingly honest in style, Gerrys comedy is enriched by a somewhat unique perspective.

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It

programme.sub-title: A Rare Bear and a German Cat

programme.title: The Yorkshire Auction House
programme.desc: A widow wants to sell furniture and artworks she once shared with her husband. Plus, a woman searches for gold lost in her later mothers flat.

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip

programme.desc: The discovery of a mutilated corpse prompts a sweeping investigation into a trio of murders, an unreliable informant and the most deadly cartel in Shreveport, Louisiana.

programme.desc: This feature-length film examines Aileens harrowing life, tracing her story from the freezing woods of Michigan to the steamy swamps of Florida, and features her closest friends, her lawyers and even her estranged birth mother. At the heart of the film are the astonishing, unique, troubled interviews Broomfield filmed with Wuornos in prison, including her last, defiant words before execution.

programme.title: Trevor McDonald and the Killer Nurse

programme.title: Conviction: Murder in Suburbia
programme.desc: Inside Justice investigate the conviction of Glyn Razzell, found guilty of his wifes murder. Glyn maintains his innocence, and now theres an alleged link with a double murderer.

programme.title: Conviction: Murder at the Station
programme.desc: Following a team of experts as they investigate whether evidence exists that could challenge the conviction of a man in prison for a murder he denies. Every year, over 500 people in the UK are convicted of murder. A guilty verdict can only be challenged if new evidence comes to light. Inside Justice is a specialist charity led by Louise Shorter, supported by a panel of criminal lawyers, ex-police officers and forensics experts who offer assistance to prisoners who are protesting their innocence.

programme.title: Conviction: Murder at the Station
programme.desc: Louise investigates a case, looking for evidence that could present grounds to bring an appeal, and ultimately overturn the conviction of a Southampton man in prison for the murder of his secret lover.

programme.title: Britains Forgotten Serial Killer
programme.desc: This is the chilling but little-known story of Trevor Hardy, the serial killer known as the `Beast of Manchester who murdered three teenage girls between December 1974 and March 1976.

programme.title: FBI: Criminal Pursuit

programme.title: Man of Steel

programme.desc: Geeky highschooler Sam Witwicky buys his first-ever car, hoping it will improve his chances with Mikaela. Imagine his surprise when his beat-down Camaro transforms into an alien robot called `Bumblebee and asks for his assistance in a deadly war.

Stylish historical epic retelling the fierce battle at Thermopylae in 481BC. programme.title: Rampage
programme.desc: Primatologist Davis Okoye shares an unshakable bond with George, an extraordinarily intelligent silverback gorilla thats been in his care since birth. When a genetic experiment goes wrong, it causes George, a wolf and a reptile to grow to a monstrous size. As the mutated beasts embark on a path of destruction, Okoye teams up with a discredited genetic engineer and the military to secure an antidote and prevent a global catastrophe.

programme.title: LEGO DC Super Heroes: The Flash
programme.desc: Reverse-Flash manipulates the Speed Force to put The Flash into a time loop, which forces him to re-live the same day over and over again. The Flash must find a way to restore time and finally apprehend his worst enemy before all is lost.

programme.title: The Death of Stalin
programme.desc: When dictator Joseph Stalin dies, his parasitic cronies square off in a frantic power struggle to become the next Soviet leader. As they bumble, brawl and back-stab their way to the top, the question remains -- just who is running the government?

programme.title: The Cinema List: Bingeable
programme.desc: On the Cinema List, join us as we explore our favourite franchises to get cosy with, from Transformers to Bad Boys 2. Snuggle down on the sofa and take your pick.

programme.title: Alfie
programme.desc: In an update of the 1966 Lewis Gilbert version, the eponymous Alfie is transposed to Manhattan and working as a chauffeur. His conquests include older woman Liz, lonely Nikki and Lonette, his best friends girl. However, he begins to learn bitter truths about himself and the way he treats women and, by the end of the film, he discovers a degree of humility.

programme.title: Superbad
programme.desc: Two inseparable best friends navigate the last weeks of high school and are invited to a gigantic house party. Together with their nerdy friend, they spend a long day trying to score enough alcohol to supply the party and inebriate two girls in order to kick-start their sex lives before they go off to college. Their quest is complicated after one of them falls in with two inept cops who are determined to show him a good time.

programme.title: Billy Madison
programme.desc: Billy is the son of hotel chain magnate Brian Madison and expects to inherit the family fortune, but hes shocked when his father stipulates he wont unless he passes 12th grade in just 24 weeks. It will be tough, since he never passed 1st grade. Determined to prove his father wrong, Billy enrols at school.

programme.title: Arthur
programme.desc: An alcoholic millionaire is threatened with losing his inheritance if he does not marry the girl of his parents choosing. Despite his dislike for his prospective bride, he bows to the inevitable only to meet a light-fingered waitress who steals his heart. John Gielgud won an Oscar for his role as Arthurs loyal but outspoken valet.

programme.title: The Golden Child
programme.desc: A social worker who specialises in missing children is recruited to rescue a mystical Tibetan child from evil forces.

programme.title: Tootsie
programme.desc: An out-of-work actor dresses in drag for an audition, only to land the female lead in a TV soap. When he becomes the most popular character on the show, he feels compelled to carry the persona into his everyday life. While bringing him phenomenal success as an `actress, life as a woman also has its problems - especially when he falls for an actress with whom he works.

programme.title: Its Complicated
programme.desc: When attending their sons college graduation, a couple reignite the spark in their relationship. However, the complication lies in the fact that they are divorced and he has remarried.

programme.title: How to Lose a Guy in 10 Days
programme.desc: Andie Anderson writes an advice column for a fashionable magazine and agrees to demonstrate how easy it is to attract then alienate a man in just ten days. She chooses Benjamin Barry, little knowing hes an advertising executive whose job rests on proving he understands women so well that he can make one fall for him in two weeks.

programme.title: Little Fockers
programme.desc: Greg and Pam have been together for ten years, and now theyre the proud parents of two adorable twins. However, money is tight and in order to keep his family afloat, devoted nurse Greg takes a second job working for a drug company. That development doesnt sit well with Jack, whose previous suspicions regarding his hapless son-in-law soon come back in full force after learning of this development.

programme.title: Big Daddy
programme.desc: A thirtysomething slacker attempts to inject some stability into his life by adopting an impressionable five-year-old.

programme.title: Daddys Home 2
programme.desc: Brad and Dustys respective fathers show up to turn Christmastime upside down.

programme.title: Jackass: The Movie
programme.desc: Johnny Knoxville and his mad companions leap onto the big screen. Expect the usual mad gross-out japes, from making snow cones out of urine to using the display toilets in bathroom outlets, and from racing golf carts over hallowed greens to tightrope walking over alligators.

programme.title: Jackass 3
programme.desc: Johnny Knoxville and the gang unleash their most insane stunts yet, from roller-skating with a buffalo herd, to playing tennis with a beehive.

programme.title: Escape to Victory
programme.desc: When ex-German international and camp commandant Van Steiner suggests a match between the POWs and the Germans, it swiftly escalates into a propaganda exercise.

programme.title: Peggy Sue Got Married
programme.desc: A bittersweet story about a 43-year-old woman on the verge of divorce who magically travels back in time to her senior high-school years. She is able to act with the benefit of hindsight, but despite her efforts, she falls for the same boy all over again.

programme.title: I, Tonya
programme.desc: In 1991, talented figure skater Tonya Harding becomes the first American woman to complete a triple axel during a competition. In 1994, her world comes crashing down when her ex-husband conspires to injure Nancy Kerrigan, a fellow Olympic hopeful, in a poorly conceived attack that forces the young woman to withdraw from the national championship. Hardings life and legacy instantly become tarnished as shes forever associated with one of the most infamous scandals in sports history.

programme.title: Jackie
programme.desc: The days leading up to the tragedy of President John F. Kennedys assassination and its immediate aftermath are chronicled as they were experienced by the First Lady, wife of the charismatic president and mother of two small children.

programme.title: The Bodyguard
programme.desc: A Secret Service agent-turned-bodyguard finds danger and romance while protecting an entertainer from a stalker.

programme.title: Black Dog
programme.desc: Released from jail, Lang returns to his hometown in Northwest China. As part of a dog patrol tasked with clearing stray dogs before the 2008 Olympics, he bonds with a black stray. The two lonely souls embark on a new journey together.

programme.title: Cookster: The Darkest Days
programme.desc: A dyslexic teen misunderstood by his family and desperate for respect, becomes a drug dealer struggling with addiction and debt while a rival threatens to destroy his world, driving him apart from the woman he loves.

programme.title: The Boy in the Striped Pyjamas
programme.desc: When his fathers job results in a family move to the countryside, young German boy Bruno misses his old friends. Roaming the grounds of the large country house he now calls home, he discovers the huge fenced facility that his dad Ralf, a Nazi officer, now manages. Approaching it, Bruno meets Shmuel, a fellow youngster on the other side of the barbed wire, wearing what appears to be a pair of pyjamas.

programme.title: The Blind Side
programme.desc: Michael Oher is a poor and undereducated African-American teenager who lived all his life on the streets. His tremendous size and quickness makes him a perfect athlete and attracts the interest if a wealthy and conservative suburban family who take him in and groom him both athletically and academically.

programme.desc: Inside his book, Harold can make anything come to life simply by drawing it. After he grows up and draws himself off the books pages and into the real world, he learns his trusty crayon can set off more hilarious hijinks than he thought possible.

programme.title: Cats and Dogs
programme.desc: Unknown to humans, all-out war between cats and dogs has raged since the beginning of time. Now the cats face defeat if scientist Professor Brody can perfect a formula that will make all humans non-allergic to dogs. The cats leader, Mr Tinkles, plans to steal the serum, but first he must get past fearless dog agent Lou who, thanks to a mistake in recruitment, guards the Brody family.

programme.title: The Tiger Rising
programme.desc: When a boy discovers a caged tiger in the woods near his home, his imagination runs wild as his life begins to change in unexpected ways.

programme.title: Ride On
programme.desc: Washed-up stuntman Luo seeks help from his estranged daughter and her lawyer boyfriend when his beloved stunt horse may be auctioned off to pay his debts. Luo and Red Hare become a media sensation when their fight with debt collectors goes viral.

programme.title: Hook
programme.desc: Grown-up Peter Pan has to return to Neverland to rediscover his true identity in order to rescue his children from the clutches of the evil Captain Hook. Peter has grown up into a workaholic executive but takes time off for a holiday in London. Once there, his children are kidnapped by Hook, and Peter must throw off his corporate image and go back to Neverland to save his children.

programme.title: Monster Trucks
programme.desc: A high school senior befriends a strange subterranean creature after building a monster truck from bits and pieces of scrapped cars.

programme.desc: The Spy Kids set off on their third adventure. Secret Agent Carmen finds herself trapped in a 3-D virtual reality video game designed by their new arch nemesis, the Toymaker. Juni must save his sister, as they battle it out against lifelike 3-D monsters, completing each progressively harder level and ultimately trying to save the youth of the world.

programme.title: Jack the Giant Slayer
programme.desc: A young farmhand gets swept into the realm of legend when he must defend his land from a seemingly unstoppable force of giant warriors.

programme.title: The Godfather, Part II
programme.desc: Vito struggles to make a living, legally or illegally, for his wife and family in 1910s Little Italy, while Michael Corleones 1950s business expansion and lust for power leads to paranoia and the destruction of his family.

programme.title: Rashomon
programme.desc: A heavy downpour surprises a priest, a woodcutter and a servant. They seek protection from the water in a temple ruin. Previously, they witnessed a crime without knowing how the crime was committed.

programme.title: The Bridges at Toko-Ri
programme.desc: A conflicted Navy pilot and veteran of the Second World War questions the role he is forced to play when he is called back into service for the Korean War.

programme.title: Robin Hood: Prince of Thieves
programme.desc: On his return from the Crusades, Robin of Locksley learns that his father has been murdered by the tyrannical Sheriff of Nottingham and embarks on a campaign of guerrilla warfare to protect King Richards throne. Fight scenes are lively and inventive, and a set piece fought under the forest canopy pulls out all the stops.

programme.title: Enter the Dragon
programme.desc: A kung fu expert is assigned to penetrate an island fortress to destroy an opium and white slavery empire.

programme.title: The Italian Job
programme.desc: A group of petty criminals plan to rig a major traffic jam to divert attention from their bank heist.

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop
programme.desc: A fast-talking cop trails his best friends killer from the alleys of Detroit to the posh galleries of Beverly Hills.

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop II
programme.desc: The hard-nosed Detroit cop from the original film returns to Los Angeles to help solve another case. This time he must direct his efforts towards unraveling the Alphabet Crimes, a series of robberies committed by leather-jacketed punks. The investigation puts him on the trail of an illegal arms dealer and his hit lady.

programme.title: Beverly Hills Cop III
programme.desc: Axel Foley and his Beverly Hills buddies investigate criminal activities at a Southern California amusement park.

programme.title: Bad Boys
programme.desc: Two wisecracking Miami cops are forced to swap identities in order to track down $100 million of heroin stolen from the police. Their only lead is a young woman who can identify the culprits, but when they track her down, she mistakes one of the cops for his partner, forcing the pair into a bizarre and chaotic life swap.

programme.title: The Krays
programme.desc: The disturbing story of the making of the infamous 60s underworld figures Reggie and Ronnie Kray - from their upbringing by a domineering mother in the deprivation of Londons East End. The gangland empire they build is characterised by violence and intimidation, and the film is unflinching in its depiction of their rise to power.

programme.title: Beast of War
programme.desc: During World War II, a Japanese fighter sinks an Australian warship in the Timor Sea. Clinging to a makeshift raft, a handful of soldiers soon find themselves in a desperate fight for survival as a great white shark circles in the water.

programme.title: Godzilla
programme.desc: Effects-laden blockbuster about a huge reptile which emerges from the sea to wreak havoc in Manhattan. When a fishing trawler is attacked by an unseen creature, and other evidence of a massive reptilian creature is unearthed, he realises that the creature is following a destructive path to New York.

programme.title: Waterworld
programme.desc: Big-budget tale of the battle for survival in a world where dry land has disappeared with the melting of the polar ice caps. One man, who has become amphibious, tries to protect survivors on an artificial atoll made from scrap metal by taking on a gang of marauding pirates. He is also intent on discovering if there is any truth in the myth that there is still some land left above water.

programme.desc: A murderer who might be either dead or alive and a shipment of rare life-prolonging deuridium create a dangerous mystery aboard DS9.

programme.title: Stargate SG-1
programme.desc: Encountering a medieval civilisation, the SG-1 team members are mistaken for demons.

programme.title: Star Trek: Voyager
programme.desc: When the Doctor travels 30,000 light-years to save the life of his dying creator, his holographic programme begins to deteriorate.

programme.title: Star Trek: The Next Generation
programme.desc: The Enterprise encounters a comet which turns out to be an alien artefact that begins transforming the ship into an ancient society.

programme.title: Primeval
programme.desc: When a young man holidaying at an adventure park is found savaged to death, Cutter and the team are called in to investigate. At first Cutter suspects a present-day animal is responsible, but later comes face to face with a vicious sabre-toothed tiger that he realises has developed a taste for human flesh. Not only must the team race to catch it before it kills again, but they must also discover who has been harbouring the cat.

programme.title: Worlds Most Evil Killers
programme.desc: After a worker found the body of Jarrae Estepp at a California recycling centre, detectives closed in on her murderer, Steven Dean Gordon, and discovered that he had killed before.

programme.title: Killer In My Village
programme.desc: Peter Wrighton: The story of the senseless killing of 83-year-old Peter Wrighton, who was targeted by Alexander Palmer while out walking his dog in 2017.

programme.title: Grimm
programme.desc: Black Claw makes a strategic move against Hank that shocks everyone and sends Nick on the war path.

programme.title: Warrior
programme.desc: Leary tries to persuade Pennys father, industrialist Byron Mercer, to employ his men to complete a cable-car track. Meanwhile, Ah Sahm is rejected by the Hop Wei and Buckley encourages Mai Ling to start a war.

programme.title: Cop Squad
programme.desc: A documentary that reveals what life on the beat is really like.

programme.title: Brit Cops: Law and Disorder
programme.desc: The City of London police safeguard the square mile at the heart of a crime-ridden capital.

programme.title: My Best Friends An Animal
programme.desc: Exploring the stories behind remarkable friendships that have formed between humans and some of the most unlikely animal companions, from a bull that plays football to a mouse family living in an English garden.

programme.title: Africas Claws and Jaws
programme.desc: Reptiles have conquered just about every habitat and have evolved a remarkable array of adaptations, making them the undisputed masters of the underworld.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: Just as Hope is scrambling to get ready for her busiest day of the year, the government comes to seize the bear cub that Hope has worked so hard to save.

programme.title: Africas Wild Horizons
programme.desc: In the Limpopo region of South Africa lies an ancient land. From rolling grasslands to rocky gorges, wildlife has learnt to make the most of whats on offer. This is the Waterberg, a land of plenty.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: When a category two hurricane slams the rehab centre, Hope and her team struggle to protect their patients in the middle of the storm.

programme.title: Great Blue Wild
programme.desc: The ocean sprawls across 70 percent of the planet and is home to as many as one million known species of plants and animals. However, it is the smallest of ocean creatures that underpins the worlds largest food web, plankton. These microscopic organisms drift through the oceans diverse habitats, triggering feeding cycles in their wake. Without them, life in the ocean would cease to exist.

programme.desc: An anomaly opens underwater, dragging a teenage boy into a flooded drain. Will everyone survive?

programme.desc: Its December and Alex, Peter and Ruth face the challenge of earning a living in one of the hardest months of the year. Theyll have to profit from their livestock, leave the farm in search of part-time work and head to the coast to reap the oceans bounty.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: Rick gets his chops around the ultimate jerk chicken made by the man behind Bristols most exciting Caribbean food kitchen. He delves into the history of the citys African-Caribbean carnival and witnesses the healing power of food.

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Sylvia and Matthew in Wokingham want a romantic garden full of scent, blooms and enchantment. However, their garden currently is anything but - its tired, messy and overgrown with little to tempt the couple outside.

programme.title: Edwardian Farm
programme.desc: Archaeologists Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn go down a copper mine, just a few hundred yards from their cottage, which was once the major source of Morwhellam Quays wealth. Historian Ruth Goodman learns the art of lace-making, visiting the town of Honiton which became world famous for its lace. The boys also go tin mining in Cornwall, an industry which survived until the end of the 20th century.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories
programme.desc: Halfway through his tour of Britains favourite foods, Rick visits a Suffolk bakery making artisan sourdough. He meets an old friend who sells fresh fish, and in Southwold, Rick discovers that George Orwell held surprising views on English cooking.

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh and team visit Hull to transform the garden of Macmillan nurse Maggie Simkiss, who cares for patients at the end of their lives, treating their emotional wellbeing as well as their medical needs. Even if someone has been diagnosed with just hours to live, Maggie says there is a great deal she can do to make their final hours worthwhile and happy. Outside work she is equally devoted to charities and also looks after her grandchildren, so her garden has been neglected.

programme.title: Flipping Profit
programme.desc: Tried and tested antiques auctioneer, Charles Hanson, pint-sized trader Melissa Downhill and Manchesters master of upholstery, Anthony Devine, head to Kendal to find some bargains they can turn into some flipping profit. The three experts rummage through its antique shops, scrap yards and flea markets for a day in the hope of each finding that elusive bargain.

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: With a rotting door and botched fuse box, Jo and Pauls kitchen in their new cottage needs some serious work. Dave Wellman is the man for the job.

programme.title: New Home DIY
programme.desc: With rotten joists, woodworm infestation, broken brickwork and dodgy plumbing, Dave has his work cut out at Simon and Lorraines new home.

programme.title: The Greatest Auction
programme.desc: A bidding war breaks out over two vintage Hermes handbags. A print by famous street artist Invader goes for 100 times what the seller originally paid for it. And the coolest skateboard in town, designed by Virgil Abloh, goes under the hammer.

programme.title: My Floating Home
programme.desc: Keith has lived and worked in New Zealand for the past 25 years. He wants to return to the UK for part of the year and go touring the waterways. Hes commissioning a brand-spanking-new semi-cruiser narrowboat complete with all the latest gadgets.

programme.title: Garden Rescue

programme.title: Love Your Garden

programme.title: Edwardian Farm
programme.desc: Archaeologists Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn go down a copper mine, just a few hundred yards from their cottage, which was once the major source of Morwhellam Quays wealth. Historian Ruth Goodman learns the art of lace-making, visiting the town of Honiton which became world famous for its lace. The boys also go tin mining in Cornwall, an industry which survived until the end of the 20th century.

programme.title: Edwardian Farm
programme.desc: It is February and the team approach the half way point in their year on the Edwardian Farm. To mark the occasion this episode explores one single day in a typical Edwardian farmers life. Incorporating a remarkable cache of letters written in the 1900s in a cottage at Morwhellham Quay, `A Day in the Life reveals the hidden stories of how ordinary rural Edwardians got by.

programme.title: Rick Steins Food Stories

programme.title: Love Your Garden
programme.desc: Alan Titchmarsh presents the garden makeover show, transforming unloved spaces for some very special people. Alan and the team are in the sleepy town of Wiveliscombe to surprise a pair of real-life Golden Girls - Roberta and Enid, who have been friends for 71 years. Robbie is 91 and Enid 86. Robbie is almost completely blind and has mobility problems, so Enid has become her care, even though she herself has just been through a battle with breast cancer. They have settled in Somerset.

programme.title: Top Gear
programme.desc: Jeremy, Richard and James explore the idea that you cant be a proper petrolhead unless youve owned an Alfa Romeo by buying three slightly used Alfas, each for less than £1,000. programme.desc: Jeremy Clarkson attempts to drive the remarkable new Nissan GT-R right across Japan faster than Richard Hammond and James May can make the same journey on the legendary Bullet Train. programme.title: Mock the Week

programme.title: Mock the Week
programme.desc: Dara O Briain and Hugh Dennis laugh at the last seven days with guests Maisie Adam, Rhys James, Milton Jones, Emily Lloyd-Saini and Glenn Moore.

programme.title: James Mays Things You Need to Know...
programme.desc: Do you really know whats happening inside your head? With the aid of some mind boggling animation and motion graphics, James May cranks open your cranium to get the answers from 100 billion whizzing neurons on questions like why men dont ask for directions, why your memory is so bad and what love has in common with Class A drugs.

programme.title: James Mays Things You Need to Know...
programme.desc: You might relish cabbage about as much as a two month bout of chickenpox, but would you consider it as a leafy long lost relative? James May does, thanks to the genius of a man who changed the world, Charles Darwin. Exactly how does Darwins famous theory of natural selection explain why we are all mutants and what war is actually good for?

programme.desc: Archaeologist Ben Robinson visits the Norfolk village of Cley next the Sea and discovers that due to Dutch settlers in the Elizabethan era, its not as next the sea as it once was. He visits the church and meets Matt Champion, an expert in medieval graffiti who has found some incredible links to past villagers.

programme.desc: Lee is insufferably smug when he wins a £1000 in a writing competition with an essay entitled `Disability in the Workplace. Tim, however, is more sceptical, and with good reason; when a journalist turns up to interview the winner, she is expecting to meet a disabled author.

programme.desc: Hard-working ambulance crews help a young woman who fell down a flight of stairs and a shivering pensioner with possible sepsis, as well as a sparky 95-year-old whos had a fall.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: On shift with West Midlands Ambulance Service who treat a young lad with a fever, a golf-loving pensioner with a heart problem and a woman convinced she has lung cancer.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: An asthmatic deteriorates rapidly in the ambulance, and the crews are called to a 30-year-old car thats been driven into a wall by a 90-year-old driver.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Crews from West Midlands Ambulance Service attend a diabetic who has collapsed at home, reassure the wife of a man with heart problems and keep up the spirits of a woman with womb cancer.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The paramedics treat a young mum with severe stomach pains, take a breathless pensioner to hospital and attend an elderly lady with dementia whose nose will not stop bleeding.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Dudley crews attend a man with heart failure and sepsis, as well as a transgender patient whos in a lot of pain from an old injury. In Stoke, a patient has a panic attack in the ambulance.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: The crews are kept busy attending a man with a swollen leg, a fisherman having a stroke and a teenager whose chest pains turn out to be something totally unexpected.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: The Mathewson family continue their pursuit of classic cars. In the home counties, Derek picks up two expertly crafted replicas of the batmobile. Meanwhile, Dave struggles to contain his enthusiasm for a Second World War Ford jeep.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Paul picks up a stunning 1961 Vauxhall Cresta, Dave drools over a Ford Sierra Sapphire Cosworth, and Derek refuses a test drive in the worlds smallest production car, the 3.5 horsepower Peel P50.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek inspects a collection of old police cars that includes a Rover P6 complete with radio, sirens and V8 engine. Meanwhile, Paul tries to figure out who will buy an old 1975 Range Rover that has been converted into a fire engine.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek swaps the chaos of the office for rain-soaked Ripon to collect a 1965 Ford Anglia with just 33,000 miles on the clock. Meanwhile, Dave enjoys the clean lines of a rare Renault Caravalle.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek is reunited with the vehicle he took his driving test in, a 1957 Ford 100E Estate. Meanwhile, Paul heads north to collect a 50-year-old MGBGT that still smells like a new car.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash: Live
programme.desc: Highlights of a special event featuring an in-person auction. Derek and Dave aim to get the right price for a 1958 Jaguar XK 150, and theres a bidding war for a 1975 Yamaha Fizzy.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans
programme.sub-title: How Much Pain Can You Take
programme.desc: Baitfish murders a person close to the team in order to send a message to Pride; a citywide hunt for the elusive criminal takes place.

programme.title: Elementary
programme.sub-title: A Giant Gun, Filled With Drugs
programme.desc: Sherlock tries to find out who kidnapped the adult daughter of his ex-drug dealer, Rhys. Watson worries about Sherlocks sobriety when Rhys suggests Sherlock was a better detective when he was using drugs.

programme.title: Ambulance Australia
programme.desc: A record heat wave has the city at boiling point, and officers respond to reports of a woman assaulted by her partner.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are sent to help a 49-year-old who has fallen in the street after a night out in Stoke, while in Dudley, paramedics visit a 5-month-old who has a temperature and isnt drinking.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder in Philadelphia
programme.desc: Louis Theroux joins the Philadelphia Police Department patrolling the most dangerous part of one of the most violent cities in America. Louis senses a society trying to contain a lawlessness born out of poverty and disaffection. Over time he sees a complex picture evolve - there are cops and robbers, guns and violence, but also an understanding between both sides.

programme.title: Honest Renovations
programme.desc: Getting to know the Loopers, a family of eight-including two sets of twins only 17 months apart; now that the twins are hitting the teenage years, the house isnt working for the family.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
Katie Piper: Locked Up in Louisiana
programme.desc: Katie meets Kimberly, who is accused of killing a woman she was dating, and finds out that Kimberlys life derailed after she got into a car accident and became addicted to opioids.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: The Most Hated Family in America
programme.desc: Louis struggles to come to terms with the infamous Phelps family from Topeka, Kansas who picket the funerals of dead soldiers killed in Iraq as a protest against an America that tolerates homosexuality. The family members, some as young as six years old, wave slogans with messages such as `God Hates Fags, Thank God for 9/11, and God Hates You at pickets all over the States.

programme.title: Louis Theroux Under the Knife
programme.desc: Louis Theroux pulls back the folds of plastic America and gets under the skin of the plastic surgery patients that crowd the clinics of Beverly Hills. Louis becomes a part of the plastic surgeons daily lives, joining them for their consultations and operations in the search for everlasting youth. As Louis tries to understand these motivations, he wonders if he too might be a candidate for a nip tuck.

programme.desc: Homeowners across Britain try to complete do-it-yourself home projects on their own. But sometimes, they need help finishing the work. Thats where presenter Nick Knowles and his team of designers and builders step in. They help homeowners complete their DIY projects gone awry.

programme.title: Pete Wicks: For Dogs Sake
programme.desc: Pete meets Keira, a dog on the brink of starvation, and gentle old Jack Russell Paige, whose owner had to make the heartbreaking decision to give her up after 15 years.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans
programme.desc: Lasalles brother, Cade, needs the teams help when he becomes a murder suspect, and cannot remember what happened; Lasalle is determined to prove Cades innocence, despite the wealth of evidence against him.

programme.title: NCIS: New Orleans
programme.sub-title: How Much Pain Can You Take
programme.desc: Baitfish murders a person close to the team in order to send a message to Pride; a citywide hunt for the elusive criminal takes place.

programme.title: Elementary
programme.sub-title: A Giant Gun, Filled With Drugs
programme.desc: Sherlock tries to find out who kidnapped the adult daughter of his ex-drug dealer, Rhys. Watson worries about Sherlocks sobriety when Rhys suggests Sherlock was a better detective when he was using drugs.

programme.title: Ambulance Australia
programme.desc: A record heat wave has the city at boiling point, and officers respond to reports of a woman assaulted by her partner.

programme.title: Inside the Ambulance
programme.desc: Paramedics are sent to help a 49-year-old who has fallen in the street after a night out in Stoke, while in Dudley, paramedics visit a 5-month-old who has a temperature and isnt drinking.

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Law and Disorder in Philadelphia
programme.desc: Louis Theroux joins the Philadelphia Police Department patrolling the most dangerous part of one of the most violent cities in America. Louis senses a society trying to contain a lawlessness born out of poverty and disaffection. Over time he sees a complex picture evolve - there are cops and robbers, guns and violence, but also an understanding between both sides.

programme.title: Michael McIntyres The Wheel
programme.desc: Michael McIntyre hosts as Harry Redknapp, Carol Vorderman, Nick Grimshaw, Andi Oliver, Tom Read Wilson, Angela Scanlon and Chunkz try to help this weeks players win a fortune.

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: In the vibrant metropolis of Montreal, Michael discovers how French and British colonial roots have influenced the citys construction, cuisine and culture. Undaunted by his guidebooks description of the treacherous Lachine Rapids, he gets a thorough soaking on a white-knuckle boat ride down the St Lawrence River.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys
programme.desc: Steered by his 1899 Appletons guide, Michael Portillos rail journey through eastern Canada continues along the Grand Trunk railway, following the route of the St Lawrence River. At Brockville, he leaves the tracks for a nautical pilgrimage through the beautiful Thousand Islands. In the port city of Kingston, Ontario, Michael visits Fort Henry and, dressed for the occasion, is entrusted to fire the naval guns that protected the nations southern border during the 19th century.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek navigates the dry stone walls of the Peak District to collect a very original 1988 Opel Manta, Dave handles a rare Ford Granada rally support vehicle, and Paul contends with a Hilux monster truck.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek takes a long drive in a 1960s bus that has had a life in the sun, Dave helps a collector trim his fleet of British classics by taking a Triumph TR7 convertible for auction, and Paul is confused by an agricultural Bonser truck.

programme.title: War Factories
programme.desc: Henry Fords plan, with the help of Albert Kahn, to build a giant factory in Michigan, covering 3.5million sq ft and including an aircraft production line half a mile in length.

programme.title: The Great War in Numbers
programme.desc: Illuminating statistics help to show how, after 1916, the imperial powers redoubled their efforts to win the war.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team visit the iconic castle of the Welsh capital city of Cardiff. The castle grounds provide the perfect setting for a journey into the past, with treasures ranging from a simple pottery jug given as a love token, to an early Hockney print found in a junk shop. The star of the day must be an early draft score of Elgars Enigma Variations, signed by the composer.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona and the team are at Minehead Railway Station where spirits are high despite the weather. As the sun breaks through, the treasures begin to arrive, including a chair said to have belonged to Henry VIII and a Butlins dance trophy. The day produces some eclectic finds: a 19th-century washing machine, a historic wedding dress worn by seven brides and counting, plus an exquisite miniature of Napoleon which is guaranteed to create a bidding war among collectors.

programme.desc: Michael Portillo heads for the Last Frontier of the United States armed with his 1899 Appletons Guide-Book to Alaska. Beginning his journey amid the snow-capped mountains and ice-cold inlets of the Kenai Peninsula, Michael finds the golden onion domes of a Russian Orthodox church along with traditional Russian food and costume and discovers that, 150 years ago, Alaska was a Russian colony.

programme.title: Great American Railroad Journeys

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce and the team return to Cardiff Castle. In this Victorian gothic setting, silver specialist Gordon Foster is fascinated by an elaborately decorated Indian flask, while John Benjamin reveals that what was thought to be costume jewellery is actually the real thing. Fiona is on the hunt for a lost 1933 penny worth a small fortune and Henry Sandon gets his hands on a piece of rare porcelain which he has been waiting 40 years to see.

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: The team sets up camp outside the iconic Parliament Building in Belfast, home to the Northern Ireland Assembly. programme.title: Kaala Patthar
programme.desc: Branded a coward for abandoning his ship full of 300 passengers, Captain Vijay Pal Singh takes up work in a distant coal mine, poorly equipped, with no sufficient medical supplies or proper facilities. When the miners and their families face a catastrophe, as water has forced its way into one of the mining tunnels, endangering 400 lives, will Vijay be able to rise to the occasion or will he `abandon ship again?
